page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB per core. For acceleration, it bundles two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a total of 96 GB of VRAM. There is no local storage bundled with this instance type.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10%. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is also in the top 10% at 161.95 GB/sec, while large block read bandwidth is strong at 308.18 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall into the average tier. For language model workloads, the dual L40S GPUs achieve top-tier text generation speeds of 368.6 tokens/sec on small models and strong text generation speeds of 35.15 tokens/sec on medium models.

Given the high-density GPU configuration paired with a moderate 12-vCPU allocation, this server offers a specialized hardware profile. It is highly cost-efficient for workloads that are heavily dependent on GPU acceleration and VRAM capacity rather than massive multi-core CPU scaling. The absence of local storage means external storage solutions must be utilized. This configuration is well-suited for machine learning inference, small-to-medium LLM text generation, image processing, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring 12 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB of total VRAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance does not include local storage. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory write speeds, alongside strong performance in image processing and small-to-medium language model inference. While multi-core CPU performance is average, the dual-GPU configuration makes this server highly efficient for GPU-bound workloads. It is optimized for machine learning inference, text generation, and data science tasks.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S is a KVM virtual server featuring 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning and GPU-accelerated workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL40S features 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs and 128 GB RAM for high-performance machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM
12 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9575F processor
128.0 GB system memory with 10.67 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory write performance
Optimized for machine learning inference and image processing
No bundled local storage, requiring external storage attachments
